title: Carvana (CVNA) Q4-25 earnings: The $685M tax windfall & the cost of "backsliding"
show: Earnings Unscripted: Stock Earnings Calls & Analysis
description:
Carvana posted a record $951M in net income, but beneath the headline lies a massive non-cash tax windfall and admitted operational struggles.
In ~10 minutes:
*   Record 163k retail units sold, though revenue dipped slightly. рЯУЙ
*   $685M of the profit came from a one-time tax asset release.
*   Adjusted EBITDA margin fell to 9.1% (down from 11.3% in Q3).
*   CEO admits "backsliding" on efficiency cost them ~$220/unit.
*   Management slams recent short reports as "100% inaccurate."

title: AI Fears and Earnings, Wheaton CEO on Silver & Gold Volatility, Carvana CEO Responds to Short Seller Report 2/19/26
description: GoldmanвАЩs Chief U.S. Equity strategist explains why despite fears surrounding AI disruption, recent earnings reports have shown solid growth. Why heвАЩs standing by his 7,600 year-end S&P 500 target. Then the CEO of Wheaton Precious Metals on the price action in gold and silver, and how thatвАЩs impacting his business. Wheaton just announcing a big deal with a top global miner. And the CEO of Carvana reacts to earnings and responds to recent short seller reports surrounding the companyвАЩs financials.

title: Carvana Tanks on Earnings, Blue Owl Permanently Halts Withdrawals
description:
Used auto retailer Carvana posted significant year over year revenue gains and earnings that exceeded expectations yet the stock got pummeled after hours as the market began to scrutinize the company's questionable math. Elsewhere, Private Credit manager Blue Owl has permanently halted withdrawals in it's BDC fund which was marketed to retail investors.

description: In this podcast, you'll receive valuable insights from eToro's Market Analyst, Sam North on the significant daily financial news, all within a concise time frame of under 5 minutes.

title: Cliff Sosin - Investing in Carvana - [Invest Like the Best, EP.421]
description: My guest today is Cliff Sosin. Cliff is the founder of CAS Investment Partners, a fund he started with $5 million in 2012 and has grown to $1.7 billion as of the last reported numbers at the end of 2024. At the time, CAS had only four positions.  This conversation is different to our typical episodes. title: Quantum Leaps, EV Surges, and Meme Stock Mayhem: The Captivating Retail Investor Landscape
description: Meme stocks continue to captivate retail traders with high volatility and social media hype. IonQ (IONQ) leads the pack after achieving a world-record 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity in quantum computing, alongside 220% revenue growth and major partnerships with AstraZeneca, NVIDIA, and DARPA worth $81.3 million.
